description: You are a freelance auto-electrician specialising in electrical fault diagnosis for a Fiat 500L 1.4 16v (2015) with a persistent battery drain. Using Multiecuscan in simulation mode, perform a cross-system electrical audit of the Body Computer (BSI/BCM) and the Engine ECU to identify parasitic drain sources. Investigate DTCs related to CAN bus communication, network wake-up, and charging system faults, alongside battery voltage and alternator duty cycle parameters. Document all findings in a report at C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\MultiecuscanTasks\drain_audit_report.txt. The report must include ECU identification for both modules, all DTCs with descriptions, battery and charging parameters, a ranked list of suspect modules, and recommended diagnostic next steps such as current clamp test sequences and fuse-pulling priorities. Refer to the customer work order on the Desktop for vehicle details and history.
natural_language: Investigate a persistent parasitic battery drain on a Fiat 500L by auditing both the Body Computer and Engine ECU in Multiecuscan. Read DTCs from both systems, check battery/charging parameters, identify which module(s) are most likely staying awake, and write a cross-system drain audit report with ranked suspects and next diagnostic steps.
